channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: ⚛️ The New Era of Air and Missile Defense | War on the Rocks
item.description: Modern air and missile defense is approaching a structural limit. The model that protected forces over the past two decades remains effective, but only within a narrower envelope than current threats demand. A new approach is required, built on fire-control-level integration,…

item.title: 🔐 Cyber Operations Aren’t Slow — Our Thinking Is | War on the Rocks
item.description: The phone rang at 3:45 pm on a Friday afternoon. We were winding down for the weekend when the caller ID lit up — it was the counterterrorism analyst in our office we affectionately called “CT Brian.” When he called, it was never good news. An al-Qaeda-affiliated group seized an…

item.title: 📍 Meet the New Host of Cogs of War | War on the Rocks
item.description: Ryan sits down at the Cogs of War mic for the last time to introduce Jonathan Panter, the new host and executive editor of Cogs of War. Jonathan shares his background, from naval officer to scholar. They discuss major defense tech issues, and Jonathan shares what he hopes to…

item.title: 🏴 While the War Continues in Ukraine, the Kremlin’s Real Battlefield is at Home | War on the Rocks
item.description: On April 1, 2026, Russian officials announced the liberation of the so-called Luhansk People’s Republic — for the third time. The first announcement came under then-Defense Minister Sergei Shoigu in July 2022. The second one, in July 2025, came from the “republic’s” own head. By…

item.title: 🕵️ Bookshelf: the CIA in the 21st century | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The mission of the US Central Intelligence Agency is extremely hazardous, writes Tim Weiner in his July 2025 book, The Mission: the CIA in the 21st century. Collecting intelligence runs many risks and can test ...

item.title: 📍 The Illusion of Reciprocity | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Trump’s self-defeating trade policy.

item.title: 🌏 China Was Ready for the Age of Anarchy | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Why turbulence will make Beijing more assertive.

item.title: 🌏 Mr Trump goes to Beijing – Views from ASPI analysts | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Presidents Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are meeting in Beijing on May 14 and 15 for the first time in more than six months. It’s Trump’s first visit to China since 2017. The two leaders ...

item.title: 🏛️ US Army recovers remains of second soldier reported missing during Moroccan exercise | Defense News
item.description: The Army identified the remains of Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, one of the two soldiers reported missing on May 2.

item.title: 🌏 No Indo-Pacific peace without industry surge and burden sharing, US general says | Defense News
item.description: Pushing industry to far-reaching frontlines — and having allies share the load — is vital to Indo-Pacific security, U.S. Army Gen. Xavier Brunson stressed.

item.title: 📍 Where Does Madagascar’s Gen Z-Led Movement Stand Now? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The country has announced a constitutional referendum and elections. Can that bring democratic change?

item.title: 🏛️ MQ-9 Reaper Replacement Requirements Stress A Drone Cheap Enough To Risk Losing | The War Zone
item.description: The Air Force’s MQ-9 Reaper successor will trade survivability for lower cost, broader missions, and mass production. The post MQ-9 Reaper Replacement Requirements Stress A Drone Cheap Enough To Risk Losing appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Both Trump and Xi Overestimate Themselves | Foreign Policy
item.description: Elites in both China and the U.S. are too easily tempted to buy into their own myths.

item.title: 🌏 Trump Has One Major Focus While in China: Trade | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.S. president is expected to downplay other issues to secure new trade deals.

item.title: 🌏 Trump, Xi Prepare for High-Stakes Summit in Beijing | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.S. president is expected to downplay other issues to secure new trade deals.

item.title: 🏴 A US and allied strategy for Greenland | Atlantic Council
item.description: After US rhetoric about taking control of Greenland exacerbated tensions between Washington and European allies in January, the US, Greenland, and Denmark opened discussions about the US presence in the autonomous territory. In parallel, the Atlantic Council's Task Force on…

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy could run out of money by July, top officer warns | Defense News
item.description: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle told lawmakers at a budget hearing Tuesday that the Navy might have to modify training and operations.

item.title: 📍 India Still Stands By BRICS | Foreign Policy
item.description: The group’s foreign ministers convene in New Delhi amid global tensions.

item.title: 🌏 For Indo-Pacific states, the Arctic’s security importance is rising | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The Arctic is no longer a distant sideshow. It is becoming a contested strategic arena, and Indo-Pacific states should start treating it that way. What happens in the High North will increasingly affect global trade, ...

item.title: 🌏 House Foreign Affairs greenlights trio of arms sale bills, including Taiwan support | Breaking Defense
item.description: The committee tanked one bill, which would have allowed any country to use Foreign Military Financing to purchase weapons through the commercial sales process.

item.title: 📍 Contractor awarded $3.5 billion to build out US Coast Guard’s Arctic Security Cutters fleet | Defense News
item.description: The five cutters to be delivered by Davie Defense will represent a new class of Arctic icebreakers.

item.title: ⚛️ 10,000 Low-Cost Cruise Missiles In Three Years Procurement Plan Laid Out By Pentagon (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: The U.S. military now also plans on buying 12,000 'cheap' hypersonic missiles as part of a larger push to bolster munitions inventories. The post 10,000 Low-Cost Cruise Missiles In Three Years Procurement Plan Laid Out By Pentagon (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 The myth of US-China stability | Atlantic Council
item.description: Washington and Beijing do not trust each other. But expect Trump and Xi to remain on message this week, saying friendly things about each other. The post The myth of US-China stability appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🛡️ The US AI health data collision: Charting the future of US cross-border data flow policy, health data, and health and biopharma AI policy | Atlantic Council
item.description: The race to control America's most sensitive data is already underway, and most people have no idea. The post The US AI health data collision: Charting the future of US cross-border data flow policy, health data, and health and biopharma AI policy appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🌍 UAE Building Massive ‘Cope Cages’ To Protect Energy Facilities From Iranian Drone Attacks | The War Zone
item.description: The move comes after the UAE has been hit by thousands of Iranian missiles and drones that have heavily damaged parts of its oil and gas infrastructure. The post UAE Building Massive ‘Cope Cages’ To Protect Energy Facilities From Iranian Drone Attacks appeared first on The War…

item.title: 🌏 There’s No Need to Fear China’s Economy | Foreign Policy
item.description: Beijing can’t easily afford to escalate any economic struggle with Washington.

item.title: 📍 L3Harris turns handheld radios into counter-drone jammers | Breaking Defense
item.description: The forthcoming Wraith Shield software update uses the existing antenna to scan for drone control signals, identify enemy ones, and jam them, L3Harris executives told reporters.

item.title: 🏴 US Army abruptly cancels deployment of 4,000 soldiers to Poland | Defense News
item.description: The Army has abruptly halted a planned deployment to Poland, bringing U.S. personnel numbers in Europe to pre-2022 levels — before Russia invaded Ukraine.

item.title: 🌍 Air Force MQ-9 fleet drops to 135 aircraft after Iran combat losses | Defense News
item.description: The Air Force is seeking to backfill the inventory of MQ-9 Reapers and field a cheaper, more expendable replacement.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force expects new KC-46 vision system in 2028 as lawmakers question price hike | Breaking Defense
item.description: Sen. Kevin Cramer said lawmakers “need more information for sure” about projected price increases for the air refueler, which budget documents show could climb by $100 million per tail.
